name: Funding — Cloud / Inference Credits Sweep
outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/cloud_credits_sweep.md exists with: (a) ≥6 startup-credit programs covered (Anthropic, AWS Activate, Google for Startups Cloud, Microsoft for Startups, Cloudflare for Startups, OpenRouter / inference-broker tier), (b) per program — current credit amount, eligibility, application URL, expected approval timeline, required applicant info, (c) one-paragraph customized application narrative for Praxis per program, (d) flag for programs that require LLC/Inc.
anti_requirements: Do NOT invent credit amounts — every dollar figure must cite the program's official page.
Do NOT include credit programs that are not currently accepting applications.
Do NOT submit any application — research and draft only.
Do NOT recommend hardcoding model selection inside the Praxis narrative (memory rule: dogfood task_type_routing).
Do NOT mention DeepSeek or Canvasshot hosting in any credit narrative; if a broker exposes those models on a no-data-sharing tier, the narrative should not single them out.

jobs.label: program_anthropic_for_startups
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/anthropic.md has current credit amount, eligibility, application URL, approval timeline, required info.
jobs.prompt:
Research Anthropic's startup credits / for-startups program (current as of today). Find the official program page on anthropic.com.

Write `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/anthropic.md` with:
- **Program name** (exact)
- **Current credit amount** (cite source line)
- **Eligibility** (stage, geography, equity-status, investor partnerships)
- **Application URL**
- **Approval timeline** (typical, if surfaced)
- **Required applicant info** (company info, founder info, use case, projections — list every field they require)
- **Notes** (gotchas: e.g. requires partner accelerator, requires LLC, requires API account already set up)

If a direct application is not the path (only available via partner accelerators), say so explicitly and list the partner accelerators.

jobs.label: program_aws_activate
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/aws_activate.md has current credit tiers, eligibility, application URL, approval timeline, required info.
jobs.prompt:
Research AWS Activate (current as of today). Find aws.amazon.com/activate or the equivalent.

Write `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/aws_activate.md` with the same structure as the Anthropic file:
- **Program name** (exact tier names — Activate Founders, Activate Portfolio, etc.)
- **Current credit amounts per tier** (cite source line)
- **Eligibility per tier**
- **Application URL**
- **Approval timeline**
- **Required applicant info**
- **Notes** (gotchas: requires AWS account, requires investor referral for higher tiers, etc.)

If there are multiple tiers, document all of them and identify which tier a solo unfunded founder qualifies for.

jobs.label: program_google_for_startups
jobs.description: Research Google for Startups Cloud Program
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/google_for_startups.md has tiers, eligibility, URL, timeline, required info.
jobs.prompt:
Research Google for Startups Cloud Program (current as of today). Find cloud.google.com/startup or the equivalent.

Write `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/google_for_startups.md` with:
- **Program name and tier** (Start, Scale, AI Trailblazer, etc.)
- **Current credit amounts per tier** (cite source line)
- **Eligibility per tier**
- **Application URL**
- **Approval timeline**
- **Required applicant info**
- **Notes**

Flag the AI-specific tier separately if one exists (Praxis qualifies as AI-native).

jobs.label: program_microsoft_for_startups
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/microsoft_for_startups.md has tiers, eligibility, URL, timeline, required info.
jobs.prompt:
Research Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub (current as of today). Find microsoft.com/startups or the equivalent.

Write `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/microsoft_for_startups.md` with the same structure as the other program files. Note Azure credit amount, OpenAI credit amount if separately listed, and any other bundled benefits (GitHub, M365).

jobs.label: program_cloudflare_for_startups
jobs.prompt:
Research Cloudflare for Startups (current as of today). Find cloudflare.com/forstartups or the equivalent.

Write `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/cloudflare.md` with the same structure as the other program files. Note: Cloudflare typically requires VC/accelerator referral — confirm whether direct applications are accepted.

jobs.label: program_inference_brokers
jobs.description: Research inference broker startup tiers (OpenRouter, Together, Fireworks, Groq)
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/inference_brokers.md catalogs available startup credit programs from inference brokers.
jobs.prompt:
Research startup credit programs from API inference brokers Praxis already routes through or could route through:
- OpenRouter
- Together AI
- Fireworks AI
- Groq

For each, search for any startup credit program, free tier for early-stage builders, or partner-accelerator credit allocation. If no public program exists, say so and note whether direct outreach is the path.

Write `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/inference_brokers.md` as one section per broker, with the same structure as the other program files. Mark with `(no public program)` and document outreach options for ones without a public credit program.

jobs.label: compose_sweep_report
jobs.description: Produce the final operator-facing sweep with customized application narratives per program
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/cloud_credits_sweep.md is the operator-facing deliverable with: per-program section, customized one-paragraph narrative for Praxis, application URL, ranked apply order.
jobs.prompt:
Read all six program files under `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/programs/`.

Produce `artifacts/funding/cloud_credits_sweep_20260501/cloud_credits_sweep.md` with:

1. **Operator context** (one paragraph: Nate, solo founder, Praxis = LLM-first autonomous engineering control plane built on Claude as core dependency, pre-revenue, US-based Bellingham WA, target external-user launch 2026-07-19)
2. **Apply order** (ranked list of programs by expected effort-to-credit ratio, with one-line reasoning per program)
3. **Per-program section** (one per file under `programs/`):
   - Heading: `## <Program name>`
   - Quick facts table: credit amount | eligibility | application URL | approval timeline | LLC required (yes/no/unknown)
   - **Customized application narrative** — one paragraph (3–5 sentences) ready to paste into the application's "about the company" or "use case" field. Mention: Praxis as LLM-first autonomous engineering control plane, dogfooding the host's models, current pre-revenue stage, planned external-user launch date, what credits would specifically unlock (compute headroom for the launch ramp).
   - **Required info checklist** — bullet list of everything Nate needs to gather to apply
4. **Cross-program flags** (LLC requirement, accelerator-only programs, etc.)
5. **Open questions** (anything that needs Nate to provide info before applying — e.g. is Praxis incorporated, what's monthly LLM spend)

Do NOT bake hardcoded model names into narratives. Frame Praxis's stack as 'routes through task_type_routing' rather than 'uses Claude Opus' even though Claude is the core dependency.
